titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 25/86

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=850 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:20:17.39869+00 | translation moa=851 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:20:16.01894+00

div.p:
. Omnibus hoc scriptum visuris . vel audituris . Rogerus filius Willelmi francisci salutem in domino . Nouerit vniuersitas
vestra . me concessisse . et hac presenti carta mea quietclamasse . Domino Roberto de Bruis . Domino vallis Anand’
et heredibus suis . pro me <et h<eredibus meis inperpetuum  duas bouatas terre . quas tenui de dicto  Domino<span data-dpt="lb" data-dpt-src="ms"> Roberto de Bruis in territorio de Anand’ versus Weremundebi  pro Excambio duarum bouatarum terre . quas
Willelmus franciscus . pater meus . de dicto Domino Roberto de Bruis . quondam tenuit ad firmam in territorio de
Moffet . In Cuius rei Testimonium  presenti carte quieteclamacionis sigillum meum apposui . Hiis Tesibus .
Domino Iohanne de Rumundebi . Domino Humfr’ de Kirkepatric . Domino Rogero fratre eius . Domino Gilberto de Ioneston’
Domino Roberto de Herice Domino Humfr’ Mauleuerer . Willelmo de Heneuille . Ada de Dunwdhi et aliis

p.cl: To all who shall view or hear this writing Roger son of William Francis sends greeting in the Lord.

Let all of you know me to have granted and by this my present charter to have quitclaimed to the lord Robert de Brus, lord of Annandale, and his heirs, for myself and my heirs, for ever, two oxgangs of land which I held of the said lord Robert de Brus in the territory of Annan towards Warmanbie, in exchange for two oxgangs of land which William Francis my father formerly held of the said lord Robert de Brus at ferme in the territory of Moffat.
In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to the the present charter of quitclaim.

With these witnesses: the lord John of Romundeby; the lord Humphrey of Kirkpatrick; the lord Roger his brother; the lord Gilbert of Johnstone; the lord Robert de Heriz; the lord Humphrey Mauleverer; William de Heineville; Adam of Dunwoodie; and others.
